action: pour milk from a small carton into an empty glass

subject.description: one person standing at a kitchen counter

objects.name: milk carton
objects.final_state: tilted back or nearly upright after pouring

scene.description: simple kitchen counter with a milk carton and a clear glass
scene.camera_view: front-facing or slight three-quarter stable view

initial_state: The milk carton stands upright on the kitchen counter. The clear glass is empty. No milk is flowing yet.

stages.description: The person stands near an upright milk carton and an empty clear glass on the counter.
The person grasps the milk carton and begins tilting it toward the empty glass.
A visible stream of milk flows from the tilted carton into the glass.
The glass is partly filled with milk. The person has stopped or nearly stopped pouring.

temporal_constraints: The glass must be empty before the carton is tilted.
The carton must tilt before milk begins to flow.
The milk stream must appear before the glass becomes filled.
The final stage must show the result of the transfer, not a reset to the initial state.
causal_constraints: Milk flow is caused by the person tilting the carton toward the glass.
The glass level increases only after the visible milk stream enters it.
forbidden_violations: Milk appears in the glass before pouring begins.
Milk flows while the carton remains upright.
The stream does not connect the carton to the glass.
The glass, carton, or person changes identity across stages.

reference_image_requirements.style: realistic photo, no text, no labels

notes: This case tests liquid transfer, source-target relation, and causal state change.
